alerte.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:alert~ causes a popup dialog to appear, with the specified message. The browser then blocks until the dialog box is dismissed. See also ~l:confirm|v~ and ~l:prompt|v~.

attache.description: [Traduction en cours] Each of the DOM variable types (~l:a~, ~l:div~, ~l:input~ etc) must be attached to an element already in the page and identified by its CSS id, before they can be used. Alternatively, you can ~l:create~ the element as a child of another DOM variable.%0a%0aThese two options allow you to build your page in HTML and attach _**AllSpeak**_ variables to the parts you want, or to build the whole structure in code and attach it to a single DOM element.%0a%0aIf you try to attach to a missing DOM element the system will throw an exception and you~sq~ll have to restart the script. To avoid this and stay in control, use the ~m:or~ clause to trap the exception and do something appropriate.
attache.examples: attache Container à/a `ec-container`%0aattache Test à/a `ec-test`or alerte `Not found`

a.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a hyperlink variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

bouton/button.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<button>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

div.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<div>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

fichier/file.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<file>~ variable, used in an image uploader. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aFor an example of uploading, see the File Manager in the [Here On The Map](https://hereonthemap.com) project.

formulaire/form.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<form>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h1.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h1>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h2.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h2>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h3.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h3>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h4.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h4>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h5.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h5>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h6.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h6>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

label.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<label>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

p.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<p>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

img.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<img>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

input.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<input>~ variable; a text entry field. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

ul.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<ul>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

li.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<li>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

select.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<select>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aThe ~m:select~ element is a dropdown list. There is no corresponding ~m:option~ element; instead, to construct the element you must create a JSON array containing the elements of the list, then use ~l:set!set Select from List~, where ~m:Select~ and ~m:List~ are the relevant variable types.

span.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<span>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

pre.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<pre>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

zonetexte/textarea.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<textarea>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

audioclip.description:
[Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<audioclip>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

See also ~l:play~.

efface.description: [Traduction en cours] Removes all the content from the named element.

convertis.description: [Traduction en cours] This deals mainly with ~l:textarea~ data that contains line breaks, which render as %d%a sequences when used in emails, for example. This command replaces them with appropriate line breaks for printing or for use in HTML. Some experimentation will usually be needed in such cases.
convertis.examples: convertis espaces dans MyText à/a imprime

crée/cree.description:
[Traduction en cours] 1. Creates a DOM element, adding it to the named parent (which must already exist in the DOM). If ~m:in {parent}~ is missing the element will be created in the ~m:<body>~ of the document.

2. Creates an ~l:audioclip~ from any reachable URL.

When you create an element it will be given an id with this pattern:

~m:ec-{name}-{index}-{id}~

where ~m:{name}~ is the name of the variable, ~m:index~ the current ~l:index|k|core~ of the variable and ~m:id~ an incrementing value. If you examine the DOM using the developer tools of your browser you will see this pattern clearly.
crée/cree.examples:
crée/cree MyButton dans Container
create Buzz depuis `https://sample.com/audio/buzzer.mp3`

désactive/desactive.description: [Traduction en cours] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:true~. See ~l:enable~.

active.description: [Traduction en cours] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:false~. See ~l:disable~.

obtiens.description:
[Traduction en cours] 1. Gets a value from the browser local storage, accessed by its key name. Browser storage is very useful for remembering settings from a previous visit to the page. See also ~l:put!put into storage~. Web storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data. If you omit the ~m:as {key}~ part the function will return an array comprising all the keys currently held in local storage.

2. Gets all the field data from the named form as a JSON string. Elements such as ~l:textarea~ will have line breaks replaced by ~m:“&0a;”~ to prevent them causing problems elsewhere, so you may need to ~l:convert~ them later on.
obtiens.examples:
obtiens Mode depuis stockage comme `mode-assisted`
get TheData depuis MyForm

section.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<section>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

historique.description:
[Traduction en cours] ~m:history~ manipulates the browser history stack. This is a complex and rather confusing subject so it's best to study examples.

~m:set~ and ~m:replace~ are identical. They 'mark' the history stack to provide a point to return to when the user clicks the browser Back button.

~m:pop~ and ~m:back~ are also identical. They have the same effect as clicking the browser Back button.

~m:push~ adds a new frame to the browser history stack.

The ~m:url~value contains the SEO string to add to the browser's address bar.

The ~m:state~ value typically contains a JSON string with enough information to restore the state to what it was prior to calling ~m:push~.

If you use ~m:pop~, don't be tempted to add any other functionality because if the user clicks Back the script won't know anything about it.

See also ~l:json parse|k|json!parse~ for dealing with URLs.
historique.examples:
historique définis/definis état/etat CurrentState
history empile url `help` état/etat State
history retour

emplacement.description: [Traduction en cours] Go to a new location. Sets the browser address to the given ~m:url~; a kind of ~m:GOTO~ for the browser.%0a%0aIf ~m:new~ is included the location will open in a new browser tab.

courriel.description: [Traduction en cours] This requests the system to create a new email, addressed to the named ~m:recipient~, with the given ~m:subject~ and containing the given ~m:message~. The user can then complete the email and send it.%0a%0aThere is no mechanism in JavaScript for sending emails directly. This is one way around it; the other is to use the REST server with this endpoint:%0a%0a~m:{site root}/static-hosting: no server email endpoint~%0a%0aThe endpoint expects a payload to be provided in the form of a JSON object with the fields ~m:from~, ~m:to~, ~m:subject~ and ~m:message~.

sur.syntax:
sur changement {list/radiobutton} ...
on clic document ...
on clic {element} ...
on clé/cle/touche ...
on navigateur retour ...
on balaye gauche/droite ...
on sortie ...
on choisi/glisse/dépose ...%0asur fenêtre/fenetre redimensionne ...%0asur reprise ...
sur.description:
[Traduction en cours] Handles a selection in a list, a click on any DOM element, a keypress or a swipe left or right in a mobile browser.

The ~m:on click document~ variant deals with the case where you have imported HTML into your page from another source. This variant detects a click in any ~m:<a>~ element as long as somewhere in the DOM hierarchy above the link there is an element with an id starting with ~m:ec-~. Any element created using the ~l:create~ command will give the new element an id fitting this pattern. You can find which link was click using ~l:document path|v![the] document path~.

The ~m:on change~ variant is used with lists and radio buttons.

The ~m:on leave~ variant allows you to take an action when the visitor leaves your page, such as writing to a tracking log. Please note that your browser is unlikely to let you take any action that will block the leave action (such as putting up a dialog).

The variants ~m:on pick~, ~m:on drag~ and ~m:on drop~ handle drag and drop. The first is triggered when you select the named component, the second when you move the selected component and the third when you finally release it at its new position. Drag and drop is the subject of a couple of examples in the Codex Tutorial, that you can see by clicking the **Tutorial** button above. See also ~l:position|v~.

See also ~l:on|k|core~ in the ~l:on|k|core!core package~.%0a%0aThe ~m:on window resize~ variant fires whenever the browser window is resized.%0a%0aThe ~m:on resume~ variant fires when the page regains visibility after being hidden (e.g. the user switches back to the tab).
sur.examples:
sur changement Countries va à/a SelectCountry
on clic HelpButton va à/a ShowHelp
on clic document va à/a ShowHelp
on clé/cle/touche va à/a ProcessKey
on navigateur retour va à/a RestorePage
on balaye gauche va à/a Next
on sortie envoie `leave` à/a TrackingModule
on choisi Component va à/a DoPick
on glisse va à/a DoDrag
on dépose va à/a DoDrop

mets.description: [Traduction en cours] Puts an item of data into browser local storage, where it can be retrieved at any time by the same computer using the same page. See also ~l:get!get from storage~.%0a%0aWeb storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.
mets.examples: mets `Admin` dans stockage comme UserName%0amets `https://google.com` dans stockage comme SearchURL

supprime/retire.syntax: supprime/retire élément/element {element}%0asupprime/retire attribut {name} de {element}%0asupprime/retire {key} depuis stockage
supprime/retire.description: [Traduction en cours] 1. This removes a named DOM element from its parent~sq~s child list.%0a%0a2. This removes an attribute of an element, such as when manipulating selections in lists.%0a%0a3. This removes an item from local storage.
supprime/retire.examples: supprime/retire élément/element HelpDiv%0asupprime/retire attribut `checked` de ListItem%0asupprime/retire `password` depuis stockage

défile/defile.description: [Traduction en cours] Scrolls an element or the browser window to the position requested. For a window, the position to scroll to, if not zero, will normally have been previously saved using ~l:scroll position|v![the] scroll position~.
défile/defile.examples: défile/defile à/a 0%0adéfile/defile Panel à/a 100%0adéfile/defile à/a SavedScrollPosition

définis/definis.description:
[Traduction en cours] The ~m:set~ command is the most heavily used in _**AllSpeak**_ (and in English generally). The examples above cover all of the different variants. In many of them, ~m:the~ is optional, but it's always best to include it as an aid to readability.

Also note that the only way to assign classes to elements is by using ~m:set attribute~. All elements are given ids when they are ~l:create!created~.

The form ~m:set {select} from {list} as {selected}~ loads a ~l:select|k|browser~ element from a json array and optionally sets one of them to be the selected value. Note that the values must be strings; if you are using numbers then add them to the list as ~m:`` cat N~ to force them to become strings.

~m:set~ is also used in other packages, for example in ~l:set|k|core!core~.
définis/definis.examples:
définis/definis le/la/les/l contenu de TargetDiv depuis SourceDiv
set le/la/les/l titre à/a `My First AllSpeak Page`
set le/la/les/l contenu de Link à/a `Click pour more`
set le/la/les/l id de ContentDiv à/a `ec-contents`
set le/la/les/l texte de HelpButton à/a `Help`
set le/la/les/l taille de NameField à/a 40
set attribut `class` de MainText à/a `normal-text`
set style `display` de ButtonPanel à/a `none`
set style `text-align` de corps à/a `center`
set le/la/les/l styles de TextPanel à/a `flex:2;font-weight:bold;margin-top:1em`
set le/la/les/l défaut/defaut de Countries à/a `Spain`
set Months depuis MonthList comme `March`

trace.description: [Traduction en cours] Sets up program tracing or acts as a breakpoint. In the former case it is given a list of variables whose values it should display. In the second case, program execution stops and the user interacts with on-screen buttons to resume or step one command at a time. Tracing is a debugging feature and requires a special DIV to be included in the page:%0a%0a~m:<div id="allspeak-tracer"></div>~%0a%0aThis is where it places its buttons and display panel.%0a%0aYou can choose whether to display variables inline or on separate lines, by specifying ~m:horizontal~ (the default) or ~m:vertical~. This follows the list of variables.%0a%0aWhen the breakpoint is hit the display panel becomes visible, showing the current values of all the variables you asked to display, followed by the lines of script leading up to where you stopped. There are 2 buttons above the display; ~m:Run~ restarts the program from the current point and ~m:Step~ advances to the next script instruction.
trace.examples: trace Value1 Value2 horizontal%0atrace Width Height Count vertical%0atrace

téléverse/televerse.description: [Traduction en cours] This command is a standard image uploader with a progress bar. The file to be uploaded will be in a ~l:file~ element and progess is monitored with a ~l:progress~ element. Progress, in the form of percentage complete, is also written to the ~l:span!status~ element given, as are error messages (if any).%0a%0aThe command uses a special REST endpoint to write the file to the server at a location starting with a directory called ~m:resources~ at the top of your WordPress installation. The ~m:path~ value is relative to that. Any intermediate directories will be created automatically if they don~sq~t already exist.%0a%0aImages will be scaled to a maximum of 1024 pixels wide or high before being saved in the server.
téléverse/televerse.examples: téléverse/televerse UploadFile à/a Path avec UploadProgress et UploadStatus

trie.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a powerful generalized JSON array sorter. Like the JavaScript function that runs internally, all the work is done by a custom function, here provided by the script.%0a%0aThe sort function sets up pairs of array elements to be compared by the comparator function, putting them into ~m:a~ and ~m:b~ arguments. The result of the comparison (1, 0 or -1) goes into the ~m:v~ argument. The example above is a complete script that you can run in the [AllSpeak Codex](https://codex.allspeak.software). See also ~l:filter~.
trie.examples: script SortTest%0a    %0a  variable Items%0a  variable A%0a  variable B%0a  variable V%0a    %0a  put vide dans Items%0a  append `fish` à/a Items%0a  append `cabbage` à/a Items%0a  append `sugar` à/a Items%0a  append `beef` à/a Items%0a  append `potatoes` à/a Items%0a  sort Items avec Comparator%0a  alert Items%0a  exit%0a%0aComparator:%0a  put arg `a` de Items dans A%0a  put arg `b` de Items dans B%0a  if A est supérieur/superieur à/a B mets 1 dans V%0a  else si A est inférieur/inferieur à/a B mets -1 dans V%0a  else mets 0 dans V%0a  set arg `v` de Items à/a V%0a  stop

filtre.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a powerful generalized JSON array filter. Like the JavaScript function that runs internally, all the work is done by a custom function, here provided by the script.%0a%0aThe filter command sets up each array element to be examined in turn by the filter function, putting it into the ~m:a~ argument. The result of the filter (true or false) goes into the ~m:v~ argument. The example above is a complete script that you can run in the [AllSpeak Codex](https://codex.allspeak.software). See also ~l:sort~.
filtre.examples: script FilterTest%0a    %0a  variable Items%0a  variable A%0a  variable V%0a    %0a  put vide dans Items%0a  append `fish` à/a Items%0a  append `cabbage` à/a Items%0a  append `sugar` à/a Items%0a  append `beef` à/a Items%0a  append `potatoes` à/a Items%0a  filter Items avec MyFilter%0a  alert Items%0a  exit%0a%0aMyFilter:%0a  put arg `a` de Items dans A%0a  if le/la/les/l longueur de A est supérieur/superieur à/a 5 définis/definis V sinon efface V%0a  set arg `v` de Items à/a V%0a  stop

demande.description: [Traduction en cours] This manages fullscreen mode, where the content of the document occupies the entire screen with now browser element being visible.%0a%0aThis feature allows **_AllSpeak_** to be used for display systems where the whole screen is used, with no title bar or other window furniture visible.%0a%0aNote that if you go into fullscreen mode you may need to provide some means of returning, as the browser Back button will not be visible.

focus.description: [Traduction en cours] Requests the focus to be applied to the field whose name is given.

BROWSER.syntax: Language features relating à/a le/la/les/l navigateur environment.
BROWSER.description: [Traduction en cours] These commands handle the browser environment, dealing with DOM objects, their properties and their behaviours.

blockquote.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM blockquote element variable.

canvas.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM canvas element variable.

fieldset.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM fieldset element variable.

hr.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM hr element variable.

image.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM image element variable.

legend.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM legend element variable.

option.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM option element variable.

progress.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM progress element variable.

table.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM table element variable.

td.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM td element variable.

th.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM th element variable.

tr.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares a DOM tr element variable.

clic.description: [Traduction en cours] Programmatically dispatches a click event on the specified select element.

copie.description: [Traduction en cours] Selects and copies the content of an input element to the clipboard.

surligne.description: [Traduction en cours] Selects (highlights) the content of the specified DOM element.

joue.description: [Traduction en cours] Plays the specified audioclip element.

rends.description: [Traduction en cours] Renders a Webson script into the specified DOM element.

{element}.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the ~sq~value~sq~ of the element. For a ~l:select~ this is the selected item; for a ~l:file~, ~l:input~ or  ~l:textarea~ it is the text content of the element.
{element}.examples: mets TheFile dans Value%0asi NameInput est Admin alerte `Invalid name`%0amets Countries dans Country%0asi Description est vide va à/a Default

existe.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the ~m:element~ in question exists.
existe.examples: si Price existe ajoute Price à/a Total

mobile.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:mobile~ has the value ~m:true~ if the system is a smartphone. See also ~l:mobile|c~ in ~l:mobile|c!Conditions~.

br.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the value ~m:<br />~, the HTML line break tag.
br.examples: mets `Line 1` cat br cat `Line 2` dans Panel

attribut.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the named attribute from an element. See also ~l:set|k!set attribute~.
attribut.examples: mets attribut `class` de TheElement dans Class

confirme.description: [Traduction en cours] Causes a confirmation dialog to appear with the prompt text given and buttons ~m:OK~ and ~m:Cancel~. If the user clicks ~m:OK~ the value returned is ~m:true~, otherwise ~m:false~. See also ~l:alert|k~ and ~l:prompt~.
confirme.examples: si confirme `Are you sure?` va à/a Continue

invite.description: [Traduction en cours] Causes a dialog box to appear asking for input. The response box is preloaded with the text given. If the user clicks ~m:Cancel~ an empty string is returned. See also ~l:alert|k~ and ~l:confirm~.
invite.examples: mets invite `Please give a reason:` avec `Rejected` dans Reason

couleur.description: [Traduction en cours] Creates a color item of the form ~m:#abcdef~ from the supplied numeric value.

docpath.description: [Traduction en cours] This is used with ~l:on|k!on click document~ to trap clicks on all hyperlinks in a block of HTML that was loaded from outside the _**AllSpeak**_ environment. The value returned is the URL minus its domain portion.

contenu de.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the content of the named element. Some elements can also use ~l:text of![the] text of~ as an alternative syntax.
contenu de.examples: mets le/la/les/l contenu de AddressField dans Address

texte de.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the content of the named element. This is an alternative to ~l:content of![the] text of~.
texte de.examples: mets le/la/les/l texte de AddressField dans Address

largeur.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the width of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~ and ~l:height~.
largeur.examples: mets le/la/les/l largeur de Image dans Width

hauteur.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the height of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~ and ~l:width~.
hauteur.examples: mets le/la/les/l hauteur de Image dans Height

emplacement.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current location, i.e. the URL of the current page.

clé/cle/touche.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the key last pressed. Used in an ~l:on|k!on key~ handler.

défile/defile position.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current scroll position of the browser window, which can be used to restore it to the same position later - see ~l:scroll|kscroll!scroll to {position}~.
défile/defile position.examples: Put le/la/les/l défile/defile position dans ScrollPos

parent.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns either the name or the ~l:index|k~ of the element inside which a click occurs when using ~l:on|k!on click in document~.
parent.examples: mets le/la/les/l parent nom dans Parent%0amets le/la/les/l parent index dans Index

historique état/etat.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current history state from the browser history stack. See ~l:history|k~.
historique état/etat.examples: mets le/la/les/l historique état/etat dans State

hôte/hote/hostname.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the ~sq~hostname~sq~ property of the current browser location.
hôte/hote/hostname.examples: mets hôte/hote/hostname dans HostName%0asi le/la/les/l hôte/hote/hostname est `localhost` alerte `Running locally!`

écran/ecran.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the screen width or height
écran/ecran.examples: mets le/la/les/l écran/ecran largeur dans W%0amets le/la/les/l écran/ecran hauteur dans H

gauche.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the position of the leftmost edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). %0a%0aIf ~m:offset~ is given the value returned is the position relative to the component~sq~s immediate parent container.%0a%0aSee also ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~, ~l:height~ and ~l:position~.
gauche.examples: mets le/la/les/l gauche de MyImage dans Left%0amets le/la/les/l décalage/decalage gauche de Text dans TextLeft

droite.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the position of the rightmost edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:top~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~ and ~l:height~.
droite.examples: mets le/la/les/l droite de Image dans Right

haut.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the position of the top edge of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). %0a%0aIf ~m:offset~ is given the value returned is the position relative to the component~sq~s immediate parent container.%0a%0aSee also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:bottom~, ~l:width~, ~l:height~ and ~l:position~.
haut.examples: mets le/la/les/l haut de Image dans Top%0amets le/la/les/l décalage/decalage haut de Text dans TextTop

bas.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the position of the bottom of the named element, relative to the viewport (window, or in the case of mobile, the screen). See also ~l:left~, ~l:right~, ~l:top~, ~l:width~ and ~l:height~.
bas.examples: mets le/la/les/l bas de Image dans Bottom

position.description: [Traduction en cours] The retrieves the position of the last mouse click, or of the position while dragging a component. The returned value is a JSON string with properties ~m:x~ and ~m:y~.%0a%0aThese values are useful when implementing drag-and-drop (see ~l:on|k~) as they tell you how far the component has been moved from its starting position. See also ~l:left~ and ~l:top~.
position.examples: mets le/la/les/l choisi position dans PickPos%0amets le/la/les/l glisse position dans DragPos

sélectionné/selectionne.syntax: [le/la/les/l] sélectionné/selectionne index dans {list}%0a[le/la/les/l] sélectionné/selectionne article dans {list}
sélectionné/selectionne.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the index of the selected item in a ~l:select~ element, or the item itself.
sélectionné/selectionne.examples: mets le/la/les/l sélectionné/selectionne index dans Countries dans Index%0amets le/la/les/l sélectionné/selectionne article dans Countries dans Country

navigateur nom.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the name of the current browser (e.g. Chrome, Firefox, Safari).

clic position.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the left (x) or top (y) coordinate of the last click event.

glisse position.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current drag position as a JSON object with x and y properties.

paysage.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns true if the viewport width is greater than or equal to its height.

choisi position.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current pick position as a JSON object with x and y properties.

portrait.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns true if the viewport height is greater than its width.

stockage clés/cles.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns a JSON array of all localStorage key names.

style de.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the value of the specified CSS style property of a DOM element.
style de.examples: mets style `color` de MyDiv dans Color

confirme.description: [Traduction en cours] Puts up a confirmation dialog with the prompt message given. If the user clicks OK the result is ~m:true~, otherwise ~m:false~.
confirme.examples: si confirme `Are you sure, ` cat MyName va à/a DoIt

has [le/la/les/l] focus.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the specified element has the focus.
has [le/la/les/l] focus.examples: si élément/element NameInput has le/la/les/l focus va à/a ClearName

mobile.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the browser is running on a smartphone.

existe.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the ~m:element~ in question exists.
existe.examples: si Price existe ajoute Price à/a Total

contient.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the bounding rectangle of the element contains the given position (JSON with x, y).

alerte.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:alert~ causes a popup dialog to appear, with the specified message. The browser then blocks until the dialog box is dismissed.

attache.description: [Traduction en cours] Each of the DOM variable types (~l:a~, ~l:div~, ~l:input~ etc) must be attached to an element already in the page and identified by its CSS id, before they can be used. Alternatively, you can ~l:create~ the element as a child of another DOM variable.%0a%0aThese two options allow you to build your page in HTML and attach _**AllSpeak**_ variables to the parts you want, or to build the whole structure in code and attach it to a single DOM element.

a.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a hyperlink variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

bouton/button.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<button>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

div.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<div>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

fichier/file.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<file>~ variable, used in an image uploader. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aFor an example of uploading, see the File Manager in the [Here On The Map](https://hereonthemap.com) project.

formulaire/form.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<form>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h1.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h1>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h2.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h2>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h3.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h3>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h4.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h4>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h5.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h5>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

h6.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<h6>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

label.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<label>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

p.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<p>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

img.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<img>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

input.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<input>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

ul.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<ul>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

li.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<li>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

select.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<select>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

span.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<span>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

pre.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<pre>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~

popup.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<popup>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aA variable type to represent a section of HTML code as a popup that floats above the page and disappears when the user clicks outside its main area. These are used primarily for menus.

zonetexte/textarea.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<textarea>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

audioclip.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares an ~m:<audioclip>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.%0a%0aSee also ~l:play~.

efface.description: [Traduction en cours] Removes all the content from the named element.

convertis.description: [Traduction en cours] This deals mainly with ~l:textarea~ data that contains line breaks, which render as %d%a sequences when used in emails, for example. This command replaces them with appropriate line breaks for printing or for use in HTML. Some experimentation will usually be needed in such cases.
convertis.examples: convertis espaces dans MyText à/a imprime

crée/cree.description: [Traduction en cours] 1. Creates a DOM object, adding it to the named parent (which must already exist in the DOM).%0a%0a2. Creates an ~l:audioclip~ from any reachable URL.
crée/cree.examples: crée/cree MyButton dans Container%0acrée/cree Buzz depuis `https://sample.com/audio/buzzer.mp3`

désactive/desactive.description: [Traduction en cours] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:true~. See ~l:enable~.

active.description: [Traduction en cours] Sets the ~m:disabled~ property of the named DOM variable to ~m:false~. See ~l:disable~.

obtiens.description: [Traduction en cours] 1. Gets a value from the browser storage, accessed by its key name. Browser storage is very useful for remembering settings from a previous visit to the page. See also ~l:put!put into storage~. Web storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.%0a%0a2. Gets all the field data from the named form as a JSON string. Elements such as ~l:textarea~ will have line breaks replaced by ~m:“&0a;”~ to prevent them causing problems elsewhere, so you may need to ~l:convert~ them later on.
obtiens.examples: obtiens Mode depuis stockage comme `mode-assisted`%0aobtiens TheData depuis MyForm

section.description: [Traduction en cours] This declares a ~m:<section>~ variable. You can either attach it to a corresponding element in the HTML or create a new element; see ~l:attach~ and ~l:create~.

historique.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:history~ manipulates the browser history stack. This is a complex and rather confusing subject so it~sq~s best to study examples.%0a%0a~m:set~ and ~m:replace~ are identical. They ~sq~mark~sq~ the history stack to provide a point to return to when the user clicks the browser Back button.%0a%0a~m:pop~ and ~m:back~ are also identical. They have the same effect as clicking the browser Back button.%0a%0a~m:push~ adds a new frame to the browser history stack.%0a%0aThe ~m:url~value contains the SEO string to add to the browser~sq~s address bar.%0a%0aThe ~m:state~ value typically contains a JSON string with enough information to restore the state to what it was prior to calling ~m:push~.%0a%0aIf you use ~m:pop~, don~sq~t be tempted to add any other functionality because if the user clicks Back the script won~sq~t know anything about it.
historique.examples: historique définis/definis état/etat CurrentState%0ahistorique empile url `help` état/etat State%0ahistorique retour

emplacement.description: [Traduction en cours] Go to a new location. Sets the browser address to the given ~m:url~; a kind of ~m:GOTO~ for the browser.

courriel.description: [Traduction en cours] This requests the system to create a new email, addressed to the named ~m:recipient~, with the given ~m:subject~ and containing the given ~m:message~. The user can then complete the email and send it.%0a%0aThere is no mechanism in JavaScript for sending emails directly. This is one way around it; the other is to use the REST server with this endpoint:%0a%0a~m:{site root}/static-hosting: no server email endpoint~%0a%0aThe endpoint expects a payload to be provided in the form of a JSON object with the fields ~m:from~, ~m:to~, ~m:subject~ and ~m:message~.

sur.syntax: sur changement {list} ...%0asur clic document ...%0asur clic {element} ...%0asur clé/cle/touche ...%0asur navigateur retour ...%0asur balaye gauche/droite ...
sur.examples: sur changement Countries va à/a SelectCountry%0asur clic HelpButton va à/a ShowHelp%0asur clic document va à/a ShowHelp%0asur clé/cle/touche va à/a ProcessKey%0asur navigateur retour va à/a RestorePage%0asur balaye gauche va à/a Next

mets.description: [Traduction en cours] Puts an item of data into browser local storage, where it can be retrieved at any time by the same computer using the same page. See also ~l:get!get from storage~.%0a%0aWeb storage is per origin (domain and protocol). All pages from one origin can store and access the same data.
mets.examples: mets `Admin` dans stockage comme UserName%0amets `https://google.com` dans stockage comme SearchURL
